message:
test(fuzz): add SymbolDistribution + CoverageEvaluation meets_policy fuzz target (resolves 2avjv)

SymbolDistribution::cached_max (coverage.rs:13-84) is an O(1) cache
maintained across add_symbol/remove_symbol — incremental on add, lazy
recompute on remove only when the removed node held the max. A
regression in cached_max distorts CoverageEvaluation::max_node_fraction_bps,
which feeds CoverageEvaluation::meets_policy — the gate that validates
an ObjectHeader.placement field. Worst case: an object pinned on a
single node passes a "max 30% concentration" check.

Existing fcp-store fuzz coverage does not touch SymbolDistribution
/ CoverageEvaluation / meets_policy.

Six properties:
- cached_max == nodes.values().max() after every add/remove
  (most-likely regression target)
- distinct_nodes == nodes.len() (zero-count cleanup works)
- add/remove inverse: matched pairs restore empty state
- from_distribution determinism
- symbols_needed monotonicity under additions
- meets_policy field-binding: tightening max_node_fraction_bps below
  actual fraction OR min_nodes above actual count MUST flip true→false

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) cached_max after add(1)+add(1)+add(2)+remove(1) MUST be 1 — the
  exact case where lazy recompute on the previous max-holder must
  surface the new max
- (b) 3-node K=2 distribution at 3333 bps with cap 3332 MUST trip
  meets_policy false (concentration-gate regression anchor)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
